reviews.review_text:
I give this game a resounding MAYBE.

This game is clever and well thought out. Not as fun as Total War. The main problem is that you will invest several dozen to a hundred hours in the game, only to have your current game destroyed by a game mechanic that NOBODY BOTHERED TO TELL YOU ABOUT. Paradox is almost totally useless at explaining why the game is doing what it does.  Answers are available on the forums, AFTER YOUR GAME IS RUINED, because you don't research a problem until you realize it is a problem. Get it on extreme sale and tool around.  Don't buy the DLCs until you know you like the game.  Don't ever buy any of it at full price.

First played as Bologna. Fun, developed a nice, powerful little country, at peace with all my neighbors.  The Pope suddenly came and took it away.  Conclusion: Don't play as a small country in Europe.

OK. Played as a small country in West Africa. Fun. Built up, conquered neighbors. Portugal came and took it away. Conclusion: Don't play as a small country anywhere else, either.

OK. Played as a small country in the middle of America. No large nations or Europeans anywhere around.  Started my first colony.  The maintenance was more than my entire budget.  Too bad nobody bothered to include that in a tool tip.  Current game deleted.

Played as England. Fun. A little confusion with how colonies behaved, but I put up with it.  Got to the Reformation. The Centers of Reformation idea is actually clever.  It forces you to attack those countries that have them, recreating the wars of religion. Of course, you don't know to do that, because what is going on is a complete mystery, on multiple levels, Why do mechanics start, how long do they last, what do you do about them, etc? Current game deleted. Province conversion, impossibility of counter-conversion, zealot armies, etc.

Maybe once you manage to get all the way through the game, and you finally figure out what the game is doing, and why, it will be better.
Paradox owns my soul after how much of their games ive played but i must say this game is way better then EU5

Is the game perfect? No. 

Is all the DLC expensive? Yes. 

Do I have more than 2K hours in the game? Yes

Will I keep playing? Yes. 

This game has the most hours in my library, it is my favorite PDX games. I think it strikes an acceptable balance between realism and play-ability. The world starts somewhat realistic but never ends even close to realistic, while this isn't prefect the reality is that to make every nation playable certain things were sacrificed and this results in crazy worlds and the ability to have interesting campaigns. Overall an enjoyable experience if you are willing to get the dlc or the subscription you will get plenty of hours out of the game.

quite fun for the first 300 years of gameplay, which are then subsequently followed by 100 years of incredibly boring continent-spanning mega-wars fought by nations boasting armies hundreds of thousands strong.
reading my old history books + learning history with this game is an immersing experience

Fadern som skriver deta, Min son tog sitt liv efter att ha kört ett mp game med furkan. säkeret 9/10 med furkan är det farligt. håll era söner och döttrar ifrån deta spel om de planerar att spela med mamadou

This game is extremely complex. Which is fine, I like complex strategy games. But the game doesn't make any effort to help you get started. The tutorials are surprisingly poor. The Youtube official beginners guide isn't better. After six hours trying to learn the basics I was just as lost as when I started, so I requested a refund.
This game has charm, but it is super-fullof gotcha moments and has absolutely horrible interface. I must be a masochist for playing it. 4/10

Game has been fun and I enjoyed a lot of the gameplay. One main thing I liked about EU4 was learning tidbits about the country I was playing and being invested in that. It does feel like that aspect is missing here but do think it will be added in later on.
Extremely interesting game.  Love the depth.   Never plays out the same way.   A game made for a brainiac like me.

So right now I end my full campaign as Byzantium. I have been spending only 1800 hours to understand what I must do. Actually, it's a really powerful nation, but you must defeat the Ottomans at the beginning of the game. 

If we talk about feedback:

+ Rule any country in the world. Do what you want. Destroy russia, burn to the ground moscow, and rename this province into 404 land
+ Build any combination of world rules. As Austin takes Ottomans, turn into your vassal, change them religion and add to HRE. Or set Hussites' religion as the official religion in HRE and convert another country to Hussites' religion. You  can also start as Aztecs and conquer all European countries. You can implement any perverted thoughts here
+ Every game is unique
+ Mods
+ Community

- In Iron Man mod, if you play as some weak country, you always will have a lot of negative events. It looks like developers don't know how to implement balance with historical focus. Instead of expanding aggressive behavior of other strong countries, the game itself simply strangles you; this is a very primitive decision.

If your internet connection briefly goes down your ironman save is no longer eligible for achievements.  So many wasted hours trying to get achievements when the game won't let you due to poor coding.
It very good. Though I am still in the early stages of understanding the game. at 1500 hours I should be better.

i love it, not sure why they are mad. i got used to getting reamed years ago. you want your  audhd game to be perfect? i cant remember the last time rome got built in a day. im obsessed with taking out the horde and taking out the muscovites

I still dont fully know what autonomy does. If you want to spend some time and learn this game, its not as hard as it looks from the outside. I recommend getting a few dlcs since the base game can be quite boring.
I can't condone Paradox's business practices and milking customers with endless addons.
Definitely not for everyone but for the people that it appeals to, it's wonderful. Most played game in my library and one I'll continue returning to.

possibly my favourite strategy game because they offer a wide range of culture, empire and dynasty to the game. Not so many games have my country's culture in it. However I still prefer playing this game with mods and cheats.

Old game, but if you want to enjoy it you need all the expantion/immersion dlc. 
Learning curve is super high and EU V has been out too for a few months. so might be better to buy EU V and learn that one from scratch.

And this game runs on Linux native !!!!

Being playing for more than the actual recorded hours before i actually own it.

You gotta love map games dud, also send help i can't stop playing 6 hours at a time.
it is amazing game, but a bit buggy. It freezes sometimes on my new macbook air for no obvious reason
Not my favourite paradox game. little overwhelming if you have never touched paradox games before, but once you get the basics down its easy enough to undersand. 8 out of 10

A good game with 10 years of content.

Also costs like 10 years of game for a full version.
güzel savaş oyunu ya ekonomi nasıl işliyor öğrenicem bi ara şimdilik altın provincelri devlesek yeter
This game is good when you have a friend that has all the dlcs and you don`t have to buy them (i will buy them eventually)
Virtualy unplayable at 2500x1440 resolution. Text and icons are so small that they are unreadable, not to mention weird font. Uless you are like 30cm from the screen.

I absolutely recommend this game. You start at the end of the Medieval period and near the start of the Renaissance Period. You can do whatever you and play whoever you want. You can dominate the world with your armies, you can cause economic collapse and trade, or marry your way to the top. 
You can restore Rome as Shinto Japan, or bring the revolution to the world. You can make peasants rule Europe or keep the Americas uncolonized. You can do nearly everything you can think of, your imagination is the limit.

the 2nd best strategy game/series i have played,also dont trust anyone that says that the albanians are weak they gave me a deep deppression until a game made them get economically destroyed and very weak
This game is honestly a one-of-a-kind game. The greatness you don't typically see. HIGHLY recommended

My first paradox game, the steep learning curve intimidated me but i had some time on my hands and i delved in deep with the help of some youtube videos. After almost 100 hours in game and quite a few restarts its slowly coming together and becoming enjoyable. Purchased on special with some dlcs, and i may even now subscribe to get the full experience.
Also have been playing multiplayer with a friend which has enhanced my enjoyment. Its a hell of a game and with the RNG i can see myself playing this for quite a few years.

Fantastic game.Despite being old and having some crazy mechanics it does still have a robust gameplay,with a ton of nations to explore,learn and even at hardest to master.Despite being hostile in ironman cases. it is a good oportunity for new players to start up dealing with the learning curve of expanding colonizing and ruling over vast areas.Moroeover this game at times can be horrendous but at times when u grasp the foundamentals and start taking achievments it could be enjoyable.With a ton of mods all the way.
very good game, consider staying on this until EU5 gets polished enough
Other then the 4 million DLCs to make your game more enjoyable (which aren't required but help a lot), the game is pretty good
This game is so difficult to get into and it just runs over you every time. Sucks.

The game is very good and you want to play for hours, but the number of DLCs is unnecessarily high and the prices are extremely expensive. They see players as money bags. Even the simplest mechanics that should be in the main game are included in the DLCs, and when you play the game without them, you don't understand much about the game and you don't enjoy it. In short, they oblige you to buy. 

MY ADVICE TO YOU IS TO DOWNLOAD PIRATES FROM GAME SITES, INCLUDING ALL DLCS, FOR FREE. DO NOT BOW TO THESE CAPITALIST THIEVES WHO HAVE NO RESPECT FOR THE PLAYER. YOU ARE NOT A MONEY BAG.

This game is beyond unsatisfactory. Does not load, redownloaded the game repeatedly, and have gone into the game files. Nothing. Highly recommend playing better games like CK3 or HOI4 and not this ♥♥♥♥.
Brilliant strategy and depth in ruling a country during a time of great development.

15 hours in and I still do not understand half the UI in EU4.

Every tutorial makes me more confused. Every achievement guide feels like advanced mathematics.

Still, I will conquer the world as Scotland, Ireland, or Switzerland as payback to the AI and the world for the suffering it has inflicted on me in EU4, once I figure out how to stop my country from collapsing, bankrupting itself, and disappearing from the map every 20 minutes.

The map does not care. Neither do I.

Veni, vidi, vici (eventually).
This seems to be the peak of the franchise, I guess. The DLC is absolutely needed, but big money. Get on sale or via... other means
My wife goes here. She said seniors can go here for free. So, I'm going to do it. I'm finally going to become a doctor.

Best game of all time. Worth spending thousands of hours on. Beware.
